records.ety: [L. adducere, adductum, to lead or bring to; ad + ducere to lead. See Duke, and cf. Adduct.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Adduced (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Adducing (<?/).]

defs: To bring forward or offer, as an argument, passage, or consideration which bears on a statement or case; to cite; to allege.
quotes: Reasons . . . were adduced on both sides.
Enough could not be adduced to satisfy the purpose of illustration.
